rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.title: Tuvan throat singing (source)
results.notes: Source-domain page on throat singing; no LLM mechanism overlap.

results.title: Parallel Decoder Transformer Synchronized Parallel Generation (2025/12)
results.notes: PDT produces synchronized parallel output streams from a shared trunk via latent workspace — directly overlaps 'shared-trunk two-stream output' and 'single-pass dual-stream decode'.

results.title: Skeleton-of-Thought Parallel Decoding (NeurIPS 2023)
results.notes: Parallel decoding of multiple streams from one model; functional overlap with dual-stream decode.

results.title: Token Assorted: Mixing Latent + Text Tokens for Reasoning (2025/02)
results.notes: Mixes latent discrete tokens with text tokens — directly mirrors 'disjoint vocabulary subspaces' (latent vs text token IDs); same core mechanism.

results.title: Metadata Diversity and Position for Efficient LLM Pretraining (2025/11)
results.notes: Explicit metadata tokens (URL/domain/IDs/timestamps) match candidate's 'structured metadata tokens (citation, tag, retrieval-link)' second-stream design.

results.title: Discrete Tokenization for Multimodal LLMs Survey (2025/07)
results.notes: Discrete tokenization survey; related but not specifically dual-stream disjoint-vocabulary decoding.

results.title: Layer Parallelism (Feb 2025)
results.notes: Parallel layer execution for inference efficiency — different mechanism from dual-stream vocabulary-disjoint decoding.

results.title: Parallel Decoding overview
results.notes: General parallel decoding overview directly conceptually aligned with single-pass dual-stream decode mechanism.

fail_reason: 5 hits >= 1 threshold. Prior art saturates the LLM-side mechanism: PDT (2512.10054) provides single-trunk synchronized parallel streams; Token Assorted (2502.03275) provides disjoint-vocabulary latent+text token decoding; Metadata Diversity (2511.21613) provides structured metadata tokens (URL/ID/timestamp) as a second stream — together these directly cover the 'single-pass dual-stream decode in disjoint vocabulary subspaces with structured metadata' claim. Skeleton-of-Thought and the parallel-decoding overview reinforce that parallel-stream decoding from a shared trunk is well-established. The 'spectral allocation / khoomei' framing is metaphorical; the mechanism itself is not novel.
